parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces substantial new surface area (retry, bigmodel-errors, glm-client, state, tracked-jobs, job-control) without evidence that the new code paths are hardened for concurrent or partial-failure scenarios. Key concerns: retry.mjs and glm-client.mjs interaction could silently double-execute operations under transient failures; state.mjs file I/O lacks atomic writes risking corruption on crash; tracked-jobs.mjs race conditions on concurrent job creation. These are the kinds of bugs that pass UAT on happy paths but surface as data corruption or duplicate work in production.

findings.title: Retry + client composition risks silent double-execution on non-idempotent operations
findings.body: retry.mjs (203 lines added) provides a generic retry wrapper used by glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed). If the upstream API call succeeds but the response is lost or times out, the retry logic will re-execute the request. For any non-idempotent operation (e.g. job submission, state mutation), this silently produces duplicate work. The retry module needs to distinguish between idempotent and non-idempotent call sites, or the client needs request idempotency keys. No such guard is visible in the diff stat.
findings.recommendation: Audit every retry.mjs call site in glm-client.mjs. For non-idempotent endpoints, either (a) add an idempotency key header, (b) wrap the call in a dedup guard keyed on request hash, or (c) narrow the retry to only idempotent HTTP methods and specific retriable status codes (not generic timeouts).

findings.title: state.mjs file writes are not atomic — crash during write corrupts state
findings.body: state.mjs (32 lines changed) persists job/tracking state to disk. File writes done via fs.writeFile without a write-to-temp-then-rename pattern mean that a process crash or SIGKILL mid-write leaves a truncated or empty file. On next startup, this likely manifests as corrupted state (JSON parse error) rather than a graceful fallback to last-known-good state.
findings.recommendation: Replace direct writeFile with an atomic write: write to a .tmp file in the same directory, then rename(.tmp, target). rename is atomic on POSIX. Add a try/catch on read that falls back to a .bak copy if the primary is corrupt.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs concurrent job creation race condition
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines) manages a collection of tracked jobs, likely read-modify-writing a shared file. Two concurrent processes (or two invocations of the companion) that both read the job list, append a new job, and write back will lose one another's additions. The diff shows no file-locking or optimistic-concurrency mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Add file-level locking (e.g. lockfile, or flock via a lock wrapper) around read-modify-write sequences in tracked-jobs.mjs. Alternatively, use atomic append for job additions and only rewrite the full file during cleanup.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may misroute retriable errors as fatal
findings.body: bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) classifies errors from the upstream BigModel API to decide retry vs. fail. If the classification logic has gaps (e.g. new error codes, changed API error format, rate-limit headers formatted differently), retriable transient errors will be promoted to fatal failures, causing the user-visible operation to fail permanently when a retry would have succeeded. The diff adds 189 lines of tests, but the test coverage for edge cases (new/unknown error codes, malformed response bodies) is not verifiable from the stat alone.
findings.recommendation: Ensure bigmodel-errors.mjs classifies truly unknown errors as retriable (fail-open) rather than fatal (fail-closed). Add an explicit test case for an unrecognized error code and a malformed/non-JSON response body to confirm the fallback behavior.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs retry backoff + BigModel error dispatch interaction could stall indefinitely
findings.body: glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) now integrates retry.mjs backoff and bigmodel-errors.mjs classification. If the BigModel API returns a series of retriable errors (e.g. 429 rate limit with no Retry-After), the exponential backoff may grow to very large values with no upper bound cap visible. A user invoking a command would see it hang for minutes or hours with no feedback, appearing frozen.
findings.recommendation: Cap the maximum total retry duration (e.g. 5 minutes) and the maximum single backoff delay (e.g. 60s). Emit a progress/log message on each retry so the user knows the system is still working. Fail with a clear message when the budget is exhausted.

next_steps: Inspect retry.mjs to verify maxAttempts and maxBackoff caps exist and are reasonable
Inspect state.mjs write path to confirm whether atomic-write pattern is used
Inspect tracked-jobs.mjs for any concurrency guard on read-modify-write
Inspect bigmodel-errors.mjs default/fallback classification for unknown errors
Inspect glm-client.mjs retry loop for total-timeout budget and user-visible progress feedback
